'''''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #51''' was one of many [[in-universe]] [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]] issues published shortly before the release of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']]. This issue was released [[April 11]], [[2002]], and is set in 13:4:11 on the [[Great ReSynchronization]] calendar in-universe, which corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].<ref name="Date">{{Hnn|51||What to Scan}} is set in 13:4:11 on the [[Great ReSynchronization]] calendar—thirteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after its starting point in [[35 BBY/Legends|35 BBY]], according to the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]]—which corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref>

==Contents==
*'''NEWS''':
**Coruscant Restricts Immigration
**Moe's Bomb Identified
**Port Crowding Increases Virus Risks
**Senators Found Gambling on Secessions
**Boon Calls Loyalists a Shadow Government
**Tanjay IV Representative Dead in Crash
**Senator Tikkes Jumps Bail

*'''BUSINESS''':
**Successful Engine Tests Cause Sienar Stock Surge
**Droid Rights Activists Decry Brilliant Missiles

*'''REGIONAL''':
**Moe Funeral Disrupted by Dug Activists
**Kabal Riots Quelled by Separatists; 4 Jedi Among the Dead

*'''JEDI WATCH''':
**Activists Deface Jedi Temple Lobby

*'''SPORTS''':
**Brawl at Regional Dejarik Tournament
**Flugg Opts for New Arm

*'''LIFE''':
**Today's Traveler - The Art of Dealing with Snivvians
**Quest for Quasar Fans Don't Like Ottekvar
**Jobal and Yari Split Up

==Development==
In the article ''Activists Deface Jedi Temple Lobby'',<ref name="Activists">{{Hnn|51|jediwatch/13411_1.html|Activists Deface Jedi Temple Lobby}}</ref> [[Thrynka Padaunete]]'s line "Who watches the watchmen?" is a direct reference to a real-world popular [[Wikipedia:Watchmen (comics)|''Watchmen'']] comic limited series, where this line serves as a slogan, raising questions about costumed crime-fighters who stand above society and its rules, much like how Padaunete views the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].
